events.title: US Strikes Iranian Ship with Drone Boats
events.desc: US CENTCOM published video footage showing three unmanned surface vessels (Corsair class) striking an Iranian ship facility. This marks a significant escalation in the ongoing naval conflict in the region.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Attacks Around Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran engaged in a series of retaliatory strikes, with reported attacks occurring around the Strait of Hormuz. This exchange highlights the volatile security situation in the crucial waterway.

events.title: US Reinstates Maritime Blockade on Iran
events.desc: The U.S. Navy-led Joint Maritime Information Center (JMIC) announced the enforcement of a maritime blockade on Iran, effective immediately. The blockade covers all Iranian ports, oil terminals, and coastal areas, impacting all vessel traffic regardless of flag.

events.title: Trump Threatens Strikes on Iran's 'Pickaxe Mountain' Nuclear Site
events.desc: President Donald Trump declared the U.S. would target 'Pickaxe Mountain' in Iran, warning Tehran to be ready. Satellite imagery has indicated recent construction at an underground facility, suggesting a potential focus on nuclear infrastructure.

events.title: UN Shipping Agency Opposes Strait Fees Amidst US Blockade Plans
events.desc: The UN's International Maritime Organization (IMO) stated its opposition to imposing fees on ships transiting maritime waterways. This statement comes after President Trump announced plans for a naval blockade and a 20% charge on cargo through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes Amid Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Following US attacks, Iran reported striking US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman. The exchange of fire underscores the heightened tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
events.desc: The UK government has enacted new powers to ban support for the IRGC, citing its links to death threats and intimidation on British soil. This move signifies a hardening stance against the Iranian military organization.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz Open Amid Exchange of Strikes with Iran
events.desc: U.S. officials maintain that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, despite ongoing strikes and Iranian claims that the strait was closed. Iran stated it launched wide-ranging attacks on US allies and bases following earlier US actions.

events.title: Iran Military Warns US Against Strait of Hormuz 'Interference'
events.desc: Iran's military has issued a strong warning to the United States, stating it will not permit 'interference' in the management of the Strait of Hormuz. This declaration comes amidst escalating naval tensions in the strategic waterway.

events.title: Lawmakers Demand Pentagon Release Findings on Iran School Strike Investigation
events.desc: Democratic senators are urging the Trump administration to release the findings of a U.S. military investigation into a strike on a girls' school in Iran on February 28. Initial reports suggested U.S. forces may have been responsible.

events.title: US Tech Industry Regulation Under Scrutiny Amidst OpenAI Lawsuit
events.desc: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the artificial intelligence company of stealing trade secrets. This development highlights the ongoing regulatory challenges and legal battles within the rapidly evolving US tech sector.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes as Regional Tensions Escalate
events.desc: Following strikes on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, the US military reported hitting missile sites and command centers, while Iran claimed to have attacked US bases. This escalation follows weeks of heightened tensions.

events.title: Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran, Citing 'Message' Sending
events.desc: President Trump stated that the US would 'probably' strike Iran again, emphasizing that any further actions would be swift. This follows recent exchanges of fire and Iran's public mourning ceremonies for its late supreme leader.

events.title: Iranian-born Engineer Convicted in US for Exporting Technology to Iran
events.desc: An Iranian-born engineer has been convicted in the US for conspiring to illegally export technology with potential military drone applications to an Iranian company linked to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).

events.title: Houthi Threat to Maritime Shipping Re-evaluated Amid Shifting Global Attention
events.desc: A review of the Houthi threat to maritime shipping in the Red Sea highlights that while global attention has shifted to the Strait of Hormuz, the underlying issues of maritime security persist. The article revisits arguments for an inadequate international response.
